claims.claim: For LiNi0.9Co0.05Mn0.05O2 Sample A (211 Ah, 342.06 Wh/kg), thermal runaway was triggered when the positive tab reached 119°C and the negative tab reached 123°C, with a critical temperature criterion of 1°C/s rate of change.
claims.quote: The critical temperature for TR is defined as a temperature change rate of 1 °C/s. The temperature of the positive tab rose to 119 °C, and the negative tab rose to 123 °C.

claims.claim: Sample A (211 Ah, 342.06 Wh/kg) reached a peak chamber temperature of 557.5°C and peak chamber pressure of 386.5 kPa during thermal runaway, while Sample B (256 Ah, 325.82 Wh/kg) reached 409.86°C and 330.5 kPa.
claims.quote: The highest temperature inside the chamber reached 557.5 °C, and the highest pressure inside the chamber reached 386.5 kPa. For Sample B... The highest temperature inside the chamber reached 409.86 °C, and the highest pressure inside the chamber was 330.5 kPa.

claims.claim: The higher-energy-density Sample A (342.06 Wh/kg, 211 Ah) exhibited a KLIB max of 99.5 kPa·m·s−1 and mass loss of 73%, compared to Sample B (325.82 Wh/kg, 256 Ah) with KLIB max of 68.2 kPa·m·s−1 and mass loss of 64.87%, indicating higher explosion risk and more severe thermal runaway for higher energy density.
claims.quote: the amplitude of the pressure variation for Sample A is significantly higher than that of Sample B... The comparatively higher explosion risk in A, as opposed to B, could be linked to A's higher energy density... The mass loss rates for A and B were 73% and 64.87%

claims.claim: Gas production normalized to standard conditions (0°C, 101 kPa) was 1.958 L/Ah for Sample A (211 Ah) and 2.182 L/Ah for Sample B (256 Ah), indicating that gas production increases with battery capacity.
claims.quote: After calculations, the gas production for Sample A is 18.35 mol, and for Sample B, it is 24.85 mol. To facilitate comparison, the gas production is converted to standard conditions, resulting in 1.958 L/Ah for Sample A and 2.182 L/Ah for Sample B. It can be observed that gas production is related to the capacity of the battery.

claims.claim: When thermal runaway is accompanied by more severe combustion, electrolyte components in the collected gas sample decrease; Sample A showed higher combustion severity than Sample B, resulting in fewer detected electrolyte gas components for Sample A.
claims.quote: When TR is accompanied by combustion, the electrolyte components in the collected gas sample will decrease. The severity of combustion in Sample A is higher than that in Sample B. The electrolyte components ejected will also be more fully ignited. The detected electrolyte components of the TR gases from the two batteries are also significantly higher for the latter compared to the former.

claims.claim: The linearized gas flow rate during thermal runaway for the high-power 18650 cell (SonyVTC5A/NCA) is 22.15 ± 14.70 gs−1, and for the high-energy cell (LGMJ1/NMC811) is 27.92 ± 9.500 gs−1.
claims.quote: Our results show, that the linearized gas flow rate for the high-power and high-energy cell is 22.15 gs−1 and 27.92 gs−1, respectively.

claims.claim: The maximum gas velocity during thermal runaway of the high-energy 18650 cell (LGMJ1/NMC811) is 398.5 ± 207.1 ms−1, following a single peak asymmetrical pattern.
claims.quote: in case of the high-energy cell, it follows a single peak asymmetrical pattern with a peak of 398.5 ms−1

claims.claim: The maximum gas velocity during thermal runaway of the high-power 18650 cell (SonyVTC5A/NCA) is 260.9 ± 103.5 ms−1, following a bumpy pattern with two to three bumps.
claims.quote: the high-power cell shows a bumpy pattern with a maximum gas velocity of 260.9 ms−1

claims.claim: The transition time from safety valve opening to thermal runaway for the high-power NCA cell (SonyVTC5A) is 69.46 ± 12.67 s, while for the high-energy NMC811 cell (LGMJ1) it is 189.54 ± 87.56 s, making the high-energy cell transition time at least twice as long.
claims.quote: It took 69.46 ± 12.67 s for the SonyVTC5A cells to go from opening of the safety valve into TR, whereas the non-reheated LGMJ1 cells needed 189.54 ± 87.56 s.

claims.claim: The mass loss during the thermal runaway stage was 57.50% for SonyVTC5A and 63.45% for LGMJ1, while total mass loss across all stages ranged from 55.88% to 81.64% for SonyVTC5A and 50.49% to 74.28% for LGMJ1.
claims.quote: The boxplots (e) and (f) underneath show averaged mass losses with the Osci for SonyVTC5A and LGMJ1 of 57.50% and 63.45%, respectively... The total mass loss lays between 55.88% and 81.64% for SonyVTC5A and 50.49% up to 74.28% for LGMJ1.

claims.claim: The expansion force of a 58 Ah Li(Ni0.8Co0.1Mn0.1)O2 prismatic battery reached a peak value of 1980 N at -13.5 s before thermal runaway voltage zero point, representing a 12-fold increase within a duration of 2.5 s starting from -15 s.
claims.quote: At the −15 s mark: Expansion force initiates a sharp ascent, reaching a peak value of 1980 N at −13.5 s, representing a 12-fold increase within a duration of 2.5 s.

claims.claim: During thermal runaway of a 58 Ah Li(Ni0.8Co0.1Mn0.1)O2 battery, the maximum ejection velocity upon opening of the safety valve was 90 m/s, occurring at the 1.5 s mark, with solid particles jetting to heights of up to 3.4 m due to incomplete combustion.
claims.quote: In the first mode, due to incomplete combustion, the generated solid particles can jet to heights of up to 3.4 m. The most intense occurrence of the first mode transpires at the 1.5-s mark, with a momentary eruption velocity of 90 m/s upon the opening of the safety valve.

claims.claim: During thermal runaway of a 58 Ah Li(Ni0.8Co0.1Mn0.1)O2 battery, the maximum flame propagation velocity was 17.24 m/s, occurring at the 1.4 s mark during combustible gas explosion, with the explosion center located 0.1 to 0.4 m from the safety valve.
claims.quote: The most intense event in the second mode transpires at the 1.4-s mark, with a maximum flame propagation velocity of 17.24 m/s... the explosion center to be within a range of 0.1 to 0.4 m from the safety valve.

claims.claim: During thermal runaway of a 58 Ah Li(Ni0.8Co0.1Mn0.1)O2 battery, the temperature measured 1 cm above the safety release valve (TE) reached a maximum value of 941.2 °C at 7 s after voltage zero, while the battery surface temperature (TS) reached a maximum of 810 °C at 100 s.
claims.quote: At the 7 s mark: TE attains its maximum value of 941.2 °C... At the 100 s mark: TS reaches its maximum value of 810 °C.

claims.claim: During the non-equilibrium phase of battery thermal runaway eruption, the ideal gas law is inapplicable due to the highly uneven temperature field, causing discrepancies between the gas quantity calculated by the ideal gas law and the actual value.
claims.quote: During this stage, the ideal gas law is inapplicable due to the complex variations in eruption. This phase remains unpredictable due to the intricate dynamics of the eruption process... During the non-equilibrium phase, however, discrepancies arise between the gas quantity determined by the ideal gas law and the actual curve due to inaccuracies in temperature and pressure measurements.
